questionHtml: Suggest reasons for the relationship shown in the graph.
markschemeHtml:
1. majority of individuals are active/walk to places if walkability is high;
    OR
    high walkability encourages the habit of walking so the coefficient ofactivity inequality would be low;
  2. with low walkability some individuals take exercise/go jogging and some do not;
  3. (with high walkability) people don’t need to drive increasing the incentive to walk;

 

Accept reasonable answer.

meta.title: Suggest reasons for the relationship shown in the graph.
